prompt: Implement a Culinary Study & Cooking Coach Workstation for home cooks and self-learners who want one offline app to practice recipe execution while reinforcing knowledge with targeted drills. Roles include Regular User, Parent/Coach (can review progress on the same device), and Administrator (local staff for setup and audits). In the Vue.js web UI, users browse locally available cooking lesson audio with cover art and playlists, pin favorites, and download segments for offline playback with a visible storage meter and an "expires in" label. During a cooking session, the user starts a guided workflow that supports multiple concurrent timers (for example, up to 6 active timers), step reminders, and one-tap step completion; if the app is closed or the device reboots, the session resumes to the last completed step with elapsed timer reconstruction. Tip cards appear contextually per step and can be configured by the Administrator (enable/disable, short vs. detailed mode). For study reinforcement, users answer practice questions tied to lessons; any wrong, partially wrong, or user-flagged question is automatically saved into a Wrong-Question Notebook where users add error-cause tags, personal notes, and favorites, then launch retry, similar-question drills, or variant drills. The interface provides clear in-app reminders for due practice and overdue items, and a local "mock checkout" flow is available for paid content bundles with receipts and a daily reconciliation export for bookkeeping, all without requiring internet connectivity. The backend uses Spring Boot to expose REST-style APIs consumed by the decoupled Vue.js frontend, persisting all data in MySQL on the same machine or local network. Core models include users/roles, sessions/steps/timers, tip-card configurations, audio assets and cache manifests, questions, attempts, wrong-notebook entries, notifications, and transaction logs. Authentication is local username/password only, with passwords stored using salted hashing (BCrypt, cost factor 12), optional TOTP-based MFA, and strict login policies (minimum 12 characters, complexity rules, lockout for 15 minutes after 5 failed attempts, and per-device session limits). Security controls include CSRF protection, XSS/injection-safe input validation, resource-level access control on every API, privilege-escalation checks for admin actions, API rate limiting (e.g., 60 requests/minute/user) with a local CAPTCHA challenge after repeated failures, signed requests with nonce and 5-minute anti-replay windows, and encryption/masking for sensitive fields such as recovery codes and payment reference tokens. Offline caching enforces a default 2.0 GB limit and 30-day validity per downloaded audio segment, with LRU cleanup and user-visible storage metrics; degraded mode disables downloads and continues playback/drills when the network is poor. Observability is built in with centralized logs containing trace IDs, job-run dashboards for reminders and reconciliation exports, success/latency/throughput monitoring for the content pipeline, anomaly alerts based on threshold rules (for example, error rate >2% over 10 minutes), and capacity assessment reports; privacy access logs record when a Parent/Coach or Administrator views a user’s notebook or history.
